goal: Land the cross-crate plugin-lifecycle integration test binary at tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs (per DEC-0010) that exercises HOT/COLD round-trip, crash recovery via the circuit breaker, and independent-runtime isolation against the real mock-mcp-plugin subprocess and real-ENOENT failure paths — closing P2-W6-F08 and the entire P2-W6 (Plugin system) feature set.
plan_summary: Add a [[test]] entry plus a ucil-daemon dev-dep to tests/integration/Cargo.toml. Create tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs hosting three module-root #[tokio::test] functions (DEC-0007 frozen-selector placement) that each drive a real PluginManager + PluginRuntime through one lifecycle slice end-to-end against the real mock-mcp-plugin binary (resolved via env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")/../../target/<profile>/mock-mcp-plugin) and the real-ENOENT placeholder /__ucil_test_nonexistent_breaker_binary__. Time-dependent slices use the existing PluginManager::with_circuit_breaker_base(Duration) builder and the pub PluginRuntime::idle_timeout field to compress production seconds/minutes to milliseconds, with dual-bound (lower behavior + upper fast-test) elapsed-time assertions per the WO-0043 lessons. Add scripts/verify/P2-W6-F08.sh that warms cargo build of the mock binary then runs the workspace-level cargo test --test test_plugin_lifecycle. Zero source changes under crates/**/src/** or under plugins/**.
scope_in: Edit tests/integration/Cargo.toml: append [[test]] name="test_plugin_lifecycle" path="test_plugin_lifecycle.rs"; add to [dev-dependencies] ucil-daemon = { path = "../../crates/ucil-daemon" } (also tempfile, tokio, serde_json, async-trait if not already present — they ARE already there at lines 91–98 per the WO-0038 layout).

Create tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs: file-level //! docstring leads with 'Mocking PluginManager / PluginRuntime / tokio::process::Command is forbidden — the WO-0046 contract is precisely that real PluginManager dispatches against real subprocesses (mock-mcp-plugin or ENOENT)' (WO-0044 prohibition shape). NO `mod tests { }` wrapper; tests live at module root per DEC-0007.

Test #1 — `test_plugin_hot_cold_round_trip`: build a PluginManifest in-memory whose [transport].command points at the mock-mcp-plugin binary path resolved via env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR").join("../../target").join(profile).join("mock-mcp-plugin"); register via PluginManager::register; activate; assert state == Active; mutate runtime.idle_timeout = Duration::from_millis(50) on the live PluginRuntime; sleep 75 ms; call PluginManager::idle_check (or equivalent) and assert state == Idle within wall-clock < 1 s; trigger reactivation via PluginManager::restart_with_backoff; assert state == Active again. Sub-assertions: (a) runtime.idle_timeout was actually overridden (read-back == 50 ms), (b) elapsed transition time >= 50 ms (proves idle window honoured), (c) elapsed transition time < 2 s (production constants didn't leak).

Test #2 — `test_plugin_crash_recovery_via_circuit_breaker`: build a PluginManifest whose [transport].command is the literal string "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_breaker_binary__" (real-ENOENT per WO-0043 lessons line 110); construct PluginManager::new().with_circuit_breaker_base(Duration::from_millis(5)); register the manifest; call PluginManager::restart_with_backoff and assert it returns Err(PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { plugin, attempts }) with attempts == MAX_RESTARTS; read back PluginRuntime::restart_attempts == MAX_RESTARTS and state == Error or CircuitBreakerOpen variant per the existing test_circuit_breaker shape. Dual-bound elapsed: >= 35 ms (5 ms × {1,2,4} = 35 ms backoff sum) AND < 2 s.

Test #3 — `test_plugin_independent_lifecycle_two_runtimes`: register TWO manifests on a single PluginManager — one healthy (mock-mcp-plugin) and one ENOENT ("/__ucil_test_nonexistent_breaker_binary__"); activate the healthy one and call restart_with_backoff on the failing one (with the 5 ms breaker base); assert healthy runtime.state == Active AND restart_attempts == 0 AND error_message.is_none(); assert failing runtime ends in CircuitBreakerOpen-equivalent terminal state with restart_attempts == MAX_RESTARTS. Proves cross-plugin lifecycle isolation — one failing plugin must not poison the manager's view of healthy peers.

Path resolution helper: a private fn mock_mcp_plugin_path() -> PathBuf that anchors via env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") (which points at tests/integration/ at compile time per DEC-0010 file layout), joins ../../target/<profile>/mock-mcp-plugin where profile is read from option_env!("CARGO_BUILD_PROFILE") OR sniffed from std::env::var("PROFILE") OR computed from cfg!(debug_assertions) ? "debug" : "release". Match the env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") anchoring pattern at tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs:218 verbatim. The helper canonicalises and panics on missing binary with a single-line operator-actionable message: 'mock-mcp-plugin not built — run: cargo build -p ucil-daemon --bin mock-mcp-plugin'.

Tests use ONLY the public surface of ucil-daemon as re-exported in lib.rs:105–110 (PluginManager, PluginRuntime, PluginState, PluginManifest, PluginError, PluginSection, TransportSection, LifecycleSection, CapabilitiesSection, ActivationSection, ResourcesSection, MAX_RESTARTS, CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS, DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES, HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS). Do NOT depend on private/internal items.

Assertion idiom: `assert_eq!(runtime.state, PluginState::X, "expected X (got {:?}); runtime={:?}", runtime.state, runtime)` so panics print the full runtime — same template as WO-0042 lessons line 92 (a). matches!() is forbidden when the actual variant value carries diagnostic context.

Failure-branch assertions use `expect_err` + a `match` arm pinning `PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { plugin, attempts }` to specific strings/ints — the explicit shape is the regression sentinel per WO-0042 lessons line 92 (b).

Add scripts/verify/P2-W6-F08.sh: shebang #!/usr/bin/env bash + set -euo pipefail; (a) cargo build -p ucil-daemon --bin mock-mcp-plugin (warmup so test #1 / #3 do not race the binary build); (b) cargo test --test test_plugin_lifecycle -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 (single-threaded per WO-0043's 'no test_support env-guard needed for this WO' carve-out — none of these tests mutate PATH); (c) on failure emit `[FAIL] P2-W6-F08: <reason>` and reference cargo build -p ucil-daemon --bin mock-mcp-plugin in the failure path; (d) operator-actionable hint if the binary is missing (path printed). Script must be executable (`chmod +x` after creation).

Honour the 'no `#[ignore]` / `xfail` / `skip`' rule (root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract); honour the 50-LOC soft / 200-LOC hard commit-size targets per DEC-0005 — split test commits if a single test exceeds 200 LOC of new code (each of the 3 tests is expected ~60–100 LOC). One commit per test is acceptable; one combined test commit is acceptable up to the 200-LOC threshold per DEC-0005 module-coherence.
scope_out: NO edits to crates/**/src/** — all required builders (with_circuit_breaker_base, with_idle_timeout, runtime.idle_timeout pub field) and re-exports (lib.rs:105–110) already exist; touching source code is explicitly forbidden by forbidden_paths.

NO new mock binaries (no `mock-mcp-plugin-fail`, no `mock-mcp-plugin-slow`). Failure paths use the real-ENOENT placeholder /__ucil_test_nonexistent_breaker_binary__ per WO-0043 / WO-0044 lessons — same dispatcher, same error variant, no #[cfg(test)]-only branches in production code.

NO edits to plugins/**/plugin.toml. The ast-grep / probe / serena manifests are FROZEN per WO-0044 lessons line 165 (fixture symbol anchors load-bearing). Any test manifest is constructed in-memory inside the test fn (PluginManifest { plugin: PluginSection { ... }, transport: TransportSection { ... }, ... } literal) — NOT loaded from disk.

NO edits to crates/ucil-daemon/tests/** (existing daemon-crate integration tests are part of the regression set, not the deliverable). Selector --test plugin_manager / --test plugin_manifests must remain green unchanged.
NO edits to crates/ucil-cli/** (P2-W6-F07 already shipped at WO-0045; this WO does not extend the CLI). The CLI regression check stays in acceptance.
NO embedding-pipeline (W8) work. NO G1/G2 fusion (W7) work. This WO closes Week 6 only.

NO mocking of PluginManager / PluginRuntime / tokio::process::Command. The single token `mock` permitted in this WO's diff is the prohibition docstring at the top of test_plugin_lifecycle.rs and the literal binary name `mock-mcp-plugin` (a real built artifact).

NO tightening of PluginManager::add return type to Result<(), AlreadyRegistered | LockContention>. Carried-forward technical debt from WO-0043 lessons line 139 stays deferred — no consumer in F08's flow.

NO new ADR. DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0010 (ucil-tests-integration crate at tests/integration/), DEC-0011 (test_support::ENV_GUARD — N/A this WO since no PATH mutation), and DEC-0005 (module-coherence test commits) cover the entire WO.
NO docs/ writes. The docs-writer phase-2-close-out pass picks up the F08 + Week 6 lessons-learned and the deferred doc-rot from WO-0045 lessons line 230.

Integration test diff touches ONLY: tests/integration/Cargo.toml (additive), tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs (new file), scripts/verify/P2-W6-F08.sh (new file), the WO ready-for-review marker, plus optionally Cargo.lock if cargo regenerates it. `git diff --name-only main..HEAD | grep -vE '^(tests/integration/(Cargo\.toml|test_plugin_lifecycle\.rs)|scripts/verify/P2-W6-F08\.sh|Cargo\.lock|ucil-build/work-orders/0046-ready-for-review\.md)$'` returns empty.

Pre-baked mutation A (idle-timeout no-op): `sed -i 's|self.idle_timeout = idle_timeout;|/* mutation: drop assignment */|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` (line ~534–535 inside `pub const fn with_idle_timeout(mut self, idle_timeout: Duration) -> Self { ... }`). Run `cargo test --test test_plugin_lifecycle test_plugin_hot_cold_round_trip` — MUST fail with assertion failure on `assert_eq!(runtime.state, PluginState::Idle, ...)` within < 5 s wall-time. Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`. Confirms test #1 actually depends on the per-runtime idle-timeout override; without it, the production 10-minute default would prevent the test from observing Idle inside the 75 ms sleep.

Pre-baked mutation B (circuit-breaker base no-op): edit `pub const fn with_circuit_breaker_base(mut self, base: Duration) -> Self { self.circuit_breaker_base = base; self }` so the assignment line is dropped (replace `self.circuit_breaker_base = base;` with `let _ = base;`) at plugin_manager.rs:861. Run `cargo test --test test_plugin_lifecycle test_plugin_crash_recovery_via_circuit_breaker` — MUST fail with the upper-bound assertion `elapsed < 2s` because production CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS = 1_000 ms × {1,2,4} = 7 s of backoff leaks through. Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`. Confirms test #2's dual-bound assertion is load-bearing — the upper bound is the test's evidence that production constants did not leak.

Verifier-extension permitted (per WO-0045 lessons line 199): if the verifier identifies a third symmetric mutation (e.g. PluginManager::restart_with_backoff body → `Ok(())` early return), document in the verification report and run; pre-baked count is 2, post-extension count up to 4 is healthy.

`git status --short` reports a clean working tree between mutation steps and at the end of verification (manifest mutations roll back via `git checkout --`, NOT `git stash pop` — same pattern as WO-0044 lessons line 178 (c)).
`git diff --name-only main..HEAD` shows ZERO files under `crates/*/src/**` or `plugins/**` (forbidden_paths invariant).

lessons_applied: WO-0042 (lessons line 80): pre-baked function-body mutation checks naming exact lines + expected-failure assertions; this WO carries TWO (idle-timeout assignment drop; circuit-breaker-base assignment drop) and explicitly invites verifier extension.

WO-0042 (lessons line 81): the existing daemon-crate plugin_manager / plugin_manifests integration tests are run as named regression sentinels in acceptance_criteria, NOT folded into a hand-wave 'workspace passes' (which is also asserted as belt-and-braces).

WO-0043 (lessons line 119): time-dependent behaviour MUST use the existing with_<config>_base(Duration) builders. Tests #1 and #2 use with_circuit_breaker_base(Duration::from_millis(5)) and runtime.idle_timeout = Duration::from_millis(50); both production constants stay at their pub const defaults.

WO-0043 (lessons line 134): dual-bound elapsed assertions on every time-dependent slice (lower behavior bound + upper fast-test bound). Test #1: elapsed >= 50 ms AND elapsed < 2 s; Test #2: elapsed >= 35 ms AND elapsed < 2 s.

WO-0043 (lessons line 110): real-ENOENT for failure-branch coverage instead of a second mock binary. /__ucil_test_nonexistent_breaker_binary__ in [transport].command; no `mock-mcp-plugin-fail` introduced.
WO-0043 (lessons line 128): pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs` should return empty before push to avoid clippy::doc_markdown rejection.
WO-0044 (lessons line 169): //! file docstring leads with the no-mock prohibition; the only `mock` token in the diff is in this docstring + the real binary name `mock-mcp-plugin`.

WO-0044 (lessons line 162): zero-source-change WO shape — forbidden_paths blanket-bans crates/**/src/**, every required surface is already public. First time this shape lands an integration test rather than a manifest landing.
WO-0045 (lessons line 199): pre-baked count is 2; verifier-extension to 3 (e.g. restart_with_backoff body → Ok(())) is permitted and encouraged when symmetric. Documented in acceptance bullet 22.

WO-0045 (lessons line 222): when the executor refactors symmetric scaffolding through a shared helper (e.g. a private fn build_test_manifest), they MUST cite the literal-construction shape in the ready-for-review note so the verifier does not have to invent the patch.

WO-0045 (lessons line 215): single test commit bundling all 3 tests + helpers is acceptable up to 200 LOC per DEC-0005; do NOT pre-emptively split a 150-LOC commit into 3 pieces unless the test setups have genuinely different contexts.
WO-0044 (lessons line 178 a–d): mutation-revert via `git checkout -- <path>` (NOT `git stash pop`); `git status --short` clean between mutation steps; restored file byte-identical to HEAD.

DEC-0007: every new selector lives at module root (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) so `cargo test --test test_plugin_lifecycle <fn_name>` resolves without selector drift. The acceptance criterion `! grep -q 'mod tests {' tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs` is the mechanical gate.

DEC-0010: tests/integration/Cargo.toml gains a single [[test]] entry; the source file lives at the repo-relative path declared in the feature description (tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs), not under tests/integration/tests/. The dev-dep on ucil-daemon is the ONLY new dependency; tokio/serde_json/tempfile already exist at lines 91–98.

WO-0043 (lessons line 132): the RUSTC_WRAPPER coverage workaround is in its 9th consecutive WO. Verifier expects to apply `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-tests-integration --summary-only --json` if a coverage-gate run is invoked. Per-crate coverage detail goes in coverage-ucil-tests-integration.md.
dependency_readiness_proof: P2-W6-F04 (circuit breakers, WO-0043) — passes=true at commit 6af9498 (WO-0045 lessons commit) per `jq '.features[] | select(.id=="P2-W6-F04") | .passes' ucil-build/feature-list.json` → true.
P2-W6-F05 (ast-grep manifest, WO-0044) — passes=true; verified by lessons-learned entry in phase-log line 142.
P2-W6-F06 (probe manifest, WO-0044) — passes=true; verified by lessons-learned entry in phase-log line 142.

All three explicit dependencies of P2-W6-F08 are met. No transitive blockers — the `mock-mcp-plugin` binary is owned by ucil-daemon and built unconditionally on `cargo build -p ucil-daemon`. The daemon's public re-export surface (lib.rs:105–110) carries every type the test needs.
